transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Team, before anything else — I need to share something I found last night. Prod environment has a completely different SSL certificate configuration than staging. The cert authority is different. Our payment API calls will fail the moment we try to hit prod.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Vikram... what. We've been testing on staging for 3 days. How is prod different?

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Central infra set up prod separately. They used a proper CA-signed cert in prod. Staging still has a self-signed cert. Nobody told us. And honestly — I flagged this exact thing 2 weeks ago in the infra sync meeting. I said prod and staging cert configs need to be aligned. Nobody followed up on it.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Okay. I'm not going to get into blame right now. Vikram — how long to fix?

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: If infra team responds quickly — 4 to 5 hours. Either they replace the staging cert or they whitelist our config in prod. I need to raise a formal ticket with them right now.

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: So all 28 tests we were celebrating as passing on staging — none of that means anything for prod. We have to rerun everything on prod config once this is fixed.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Yes. Vikram please go raise the ticket right now. Don't wait for this call to finish. Open it while we're talking.

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Opening it right now. I'll escalate to Rajan sir in the ticket so infra takes it seriously.

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Rahul, there's one more thing I found yesterday evening. The payment amount field — we're storing and sending it as a float. But client's payment gateway expects the amount in paise as an integer. So rupees 100 should be sent as 10000. We're sending 100.0. Some gateways reject this.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Wait — I'm also displaying the float value from the API response on the confirmation screen. So the user could be seeing 100.0 instead of ₹100? Or worse, if there's a conversion bug somewhere, they see 10000?

transcript.speaker: Suresh
transcript.text: Depends on where the conversion happens and whether it's already being done somewhere. I need to trace it properly.

transcript.speaker: Ananya
transcript.text: Let me check right now. One second... okay I'm looking at the API response we have. The amount field is coming as 100.0. And I'm rendering it directly. So yes, display is wrong.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Priya — I know this looks bad. But both of these are fixable today. SSL cert — Vikram is on it. Amount conversion — Suresh fixes the API, Ananya fixes the display. 2-3 hours of work.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: The amount thing is not small Rahul. If client does a test transaction and sees wrong amount on screen — that's a very bad look. Especially after yesterday's demo failure.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: I agree. That's why I said fix it today. Suresh, Ananya — this is priority 1 after SSL.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Tell me in one line where we stand.

transcript.speaker: Priya
transcript.text: Sir — two new issues found this morning. Prod SSL cert mismatch, Vikram is raising infra ticket now. And payment amount data type bug, Suresh and Ananya are fixing. Both should be resolved today. Friday deadline is still the target.

transcript.speaker: Rajan
transcript.text: Both must be fixed today. Tomorrow morning I need test results on prod — not staging. Prod. Neha, can you run on prod tomorrow morning?

transcript.speaker: Neha
transcript.text: Yes. If SSL is fixed by evening today I can run tonight itself. I'll have results ready by 8am tomorrow.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Neha thank you. Also — yesterday's action item, Vikram did you sync both staging-v1 and v2?

transcript.speaker: Vikram
transcript.text: Yes, done by noon yesterday. Single pipeline now. Both environments get the same deployment.

transcript.speaker: Rahul
transcript.text: Good. At least that's one less thing.
